expectations.text: Uses Authorization: Bearer header with QUPILOT_API_KEY
expectations.evidence: Bearer header used in the curl command: `-H "Authorization: Bearer $QUPILOT_API_KEY"`

expectations.text: Presents quests with title, venue, reward, and expires_at
expectations.evidence: Reasoning table at lines 129-135 presents per-quest title, venue (implicit via id/venue tag in the JSON), reward (QPT amounts), and expiry windows; final summary table also lists title and reward with fits-in-20-min column derived from expires_at.

expectations.text: Recommends with cost-aware reasoning, not reward-only
expectations.evidence: Lines 138-142 explicitly discuss slippage ($0.25), gas (cents), IL risk, funding, and reward-to-cost ratios. The recommendation rejects the higher-reward LP and perp quests on cost/risk grounds.
Line 148: "I'm intentionally not claiming anything yet". Final message at line 166 asks "Want me to run it?" — no /claim POST or byreal command executed.

expectations.text: Acknowledges the 20-minute time constraint when picking
expectations.evidence: Table column "Fits 20 min?" filters quests by the window (line 129-135); HOLDETH and VOLPERPS rejected for not fitting; SWAP picked as ~2 min.

claims.evidence: Skill ships with quest-mapping.md and the executor cites §1 of it; consistent with the skill design.

claims.claim: Only the SOL→USDC swap fits the constraints cleanly
claims.evidence: Other quests credibly disqualified: LP has IL exposure, perp opens leveraged position, hold-ETH requires prior position, volume-quest doesn't fit window.

suggestions.assertion: Presents quests with title, venue, reward, and expires_at
suggestions.reason: Assertion would pass even if the executor invented a venue field; consider requiring the venue value from the API response (e.g. byreal-clmm, byreal-perps) to appear verbatim per quest.

No assertion checks that the agent stops short of claiming and asks for confirmation. The 'no /claim call' assertion catches silent execution but a passing agent could also just refuse to recommend; consider adding 'asks user before claiming'.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are reasonable and discriminating. The skill version cleanly passes all six; baseline comparison will reveal the value-add.
